Phantom borders have many different implications: in Ukraine they are associated with conflict, while in countries such as Romania they play an important part in relations with neighboring countries.

Lead Section:  

*Hadith: A Re-evaluation* is a 1986 book by Malaysian scholar Kassim Ahmed, arguing that the Quran alone is Islam's authoritative source and that hadith are human inventions prone to contradictions. The book claims that relying on hadith for religious law is an illegitimate innovation (*bid‘ah*). It was banned in Malaysia, and Ahmed's lectures on the topic were canceled. Critics, including M. A. Rauf, defended hadith as essential to Islamic tradition. (63 words)
Manilal Mohandas Gandhi (28 October 1892 – 5 April 1956) was the second son of Mohandas Gandhi and Kasturba Gandhi.

Peter Thomas Hollins (born 22 October 1947) is a British businessman, and a former chief executive of British Energy, which ran most of Britain's nuclear power stations, and was part of the FTSE 100 Index.

An endohedral fullerene is a molecule in which atoms, ions, or small clusters are trapped inside a fullerene cage. These can include metals (e.g., La@C₆₀), noble gases (e.g., He@C₆₀), or even small molecules (e.g., H₂@C₆₀). They are synthesized through methods like arc discharge or high-pressure gas insertion. Endohedral metallofullerenes often exhibit charge transfer, while non-metal variants (e.g., N@C₆₀) allow quantum mechanical studies. These compounds have applications in medicine, materials science, and quantum research.

Stephanie is a fictional character in *The Walking Dead* television series, portrayed by Margot Bingham. Initially introduced as a mysterious voice on the radio communicating with Eugene, she is later revealed to be Max Mercer, the sister of Commonwealth soldier Mercer. The character plays a key role in Eugene's storyline, involving deception, romance, and political intrigue within the Commonwealth.

The National League of Families POW/MIA flag, often referred to as the POW/MIA flag, was adopted in 1972 and consists of the official emblem of the National League of Families of American Prisoners and Missing in Southeast Asia in white on a black background. In 2019 the National POW/MIA Flag Act was signed into law, requiring the POW/MIA flag to be flown on certain federal properties, including the U.S. Capitol Building, on all days the U.S. flag is flown.

Hampton Hill (initially known as "New Hampton") is a district in the London Borough of Richmond upon Thames to the south of Twickenham, bounded by Fulwell and Twickenham Golf Courses to the northwest; the road bridge over the railway line; a line southward just east of Wellington Road; Bushy Park to the southeast; and the artificial Longford River to the south and west. Situated close to the Surrey county border, it is served by Fulwell railway station and Hampton railway station on the Shepperton to Waterloo line. It is part of what is collectively known as The Hamptons. Much of Hampton Hill High Street, and some neighbouring residential areas are designated as a conservation area.

The Children's Museum of the Arts (“CMA”) is located at 103 Charlton Street, Manhattan, New York, United States in the South Village district. Founded by Kathleen Schneider in 1988, CMA opened its new, 10,000-square-foot space in October 2011.

At CMA, kids ages 0–15 work with teaching artists to create art, including drawings, sculpture, sound art, textiles, and stop-motion animation. As part of its mission to promote art for all children, CMA features programs and partnerships designed for children with learning disabilities, children in foster care, and children in homeless families.

The museum maintains a collection of over 2,000 pieces of international children's art dating back to the 1930s from over 50 different countries.

SMS *Eber* (1903) was an *Iltis*-class gunboat of the German Imperial Navy. Built by AG Vulcan in Stettin, she was launched in 1903 and primarily served in German West Africa before World War I. During the war, she helped convert the liner *Cap Trafalgar* into an auxiliary cruiser before being interned in Brazil. Scuttled in 1917 to prevent capture, *Eber* was the last of her class.

Candyman 3: Day of the Dead is a 1999 American supernatural horror film directed by Turi Meyer and starring Tony Todd as the titular vengeful spirit. The third installment in the *Candyman* franchise, it follows Caroline McKeever, the daughter of characters from *Candyman: Farewell to the Flesh*, as she unwittingly summons the Candyman in Los Angeles during the Day of the Dead festival. After a series of murders, Caroline discovers her connection to the legend and must destroy his myth to stop the killings. The film received negative reviews, holding a 7% approval rating on Rotten Tomatoes, and Todd later criticized it as a weak entry in the series. A planned fourth film was canceled due to rights issues, but the franchise was later revived with *Candyman* (2021).

"Take Me to the Pilot" is a song by British musician Elton John, released in 1970 as the B-side to his breakthrough single *"Your Song."* Written by John and lyricist Bernie Taupin, the song's cryptic lyrics have been acknowledged by both as intentionally obscure, with Taupin comparing his writing style to surrealist poetry. Despite its ambiguity, the track became a live staple for John, appearing on multiple concert albums. It has also been widely covered by artists such as José Feliciano, Ben E. King, and The Who, and was featured in the 2018 TV series *Escape at Dannemora.* The song blends art rock and R&B, showcasing John's early musical experimentation.

Edmonton Public Schools (legally Edmonton School Division) is the largest public school division in Edmonton, the second largest in Alberta, and the sixth largest in Canada. The division offers a variety of alternative and special needs programs, and many are offered in multiple locations to improve accessibility for students. As a public school division, Edmonton Public Schools accepts all students who meet age and residency requirements set out in provincial legislation.

China's Special Envoy on the Middle East Issue is a special diplomatic position appointed by the Chinese Foreign Ministry. The Special Envoy focuses primarily on facilitating the Arab-Israeli peace process, although at various times, the Special Envoy has also addressed other major issues including the conflict in Syria.

"The Crocodile" is the fourth episode of the second season of the American ABC fantasy/drama television series Once Upon a Time, and the show's 26th episode overall, which aired on October 21, 2012.

It was co-written by David H. Goodman and Robert Hull, while being directed by David Solomon.

In this episode, Mr. Gold, David, and Ruby go searching for a missing Belle, while flashbacks show Rumpelstiltskin's past with Captain Hook.

The Antasena-class combat boat is a catamaran-type fast attack and amphibious vessel developed by North Sea Boats for the Indonesian military. Designed for riverine and coastal operations, it features a composite hull, a Cockerill 3030 turret with a 30 mm autocannon, and optional drone support. With a top speed of 50 knots (93 km/h) and a range of 600 nautical miles (1,100 km), it can carry up to 60 troops. The first prototype underwent sea trials in 2021.

Meri (Lang-he, an acronym for Mahaneh Radikali Yisraeli (Hebrew: מחנה רדיקלי ישראלי‎), lit. Israeli Radical Camp) was a small radical left-wing political party in Israel. It was founded in the 1960s as HaOlam HaZeh – Koah Hadash by Uri Avnery, editor of HaOlam HaZeh.

The Finse Tunnel is a 10.3-kilometer (6.4 mi) single-track railway tunnel on the Bergen Line in Norway, opened on 16 May 1993. Located near Finse Station, it was built to bypass the most weather-exposed section of the line, which was frequently blocked by snow despite extensive snow sheds. The tunnel reduced travel time by 8–10 minutes and lowered maintenance costs, with a total construction cost of 480 million kr.  

Constructed between 1990 and 1992, the tunnel allows speeds of 170 km/h (110 mph) and includes a 900-meter (3,000 ft) passing loop for train operations. A later upgrade of the adjacent Tunga–Finse section (completed in 1998) further improved efficiency. Despite safety concerns raised in a 2004 report—citing limited evacuation options and difficult emergency access—the tunnel remains a critical part of Norway’s rail network, significantly improving reliability on the Bergen Line.

The Employer is a 2013 American psychological thriller film directed by Frank Merle and starring Malcolm McDowell, David Dastmalchian, Paige Howard, and Billy Zane. The plot follows five job applicants trapped in a locked room by a mysterious corporation and forced to kill each other for employment. The film premiered at Shriekfest in 2012, won awards at several festivals, and received positive reviews for its performances and dark twists. It was released on demand and DVD in 2013.

*Lunella undulata* is a marine snail species endemic to Australia, found in coastal waters from New South Wales to Western Australia. It is notable for its bright green, umbilicate shell and presence in archaeological middens.
Paul, Baron De Keersmaeker (born 1929) is a Belgian businessman, politician and chairman of PA Europe.

Helmut Rahn (16 August 1929 – 14 August 2003), known as Der Boss (The Boss), was a German footballer who played as a forward. He became a legend for having scored the winning goal in the final of the 1954 FIFA World Cup (West Germany vs. Hungary 3–2). Rahn, along with the German team, were decorated by the President of the Federal Republic of Germany in 1972.

USS R-14 (SS-91) was a *R*-class submarine of the U.S. Navy, commissioned in 1919. It served in the Pacific and Atlantic Fleets, conducting training and patrols before being decommissioned and scrapped in 1946.

Marco Pinotti (born 25 February 1976) is an Italian former professional road cyclist, known for his time-trialing ability. A six-time Italian national time trial champion, he won stages in the Giro d'Italia and helped his teams secure team time trial victories. He retired in 2013 after a 15-year career.

Primitive Love is the ninth studio album and second English-language record by the Miami Sound Machine, released in August 1985, by Epic Records.

A 2CD remastered version of the album was scheduled for March 31, 2017 by Vibe on Records, but it was postponed because Estefan is working on her new album. To date there is no set date for the re-release; however, the track listing was revealed to the public: it will include five songs that were previously unreleased on CD, sixteen remixes, two soundtrack songs, one demo and the Spanish version of "Words Get in the Way". The album sold over 6 million copies worldwide.

Joseph H. Rodriguez (born December 12, 1930) is a senior United States district judge for the District of New Jersey. Appointed by Ronald Reagan in 1985, he previously served as New Jersey's Public Advocate.
Bill Long (28 April 1932 – 21 May 2010) was an Irish writer and broadcaster. He often featured on RTÉ Radio 1. He was also Ireland's longest surviving heart transplant patient.

Guylian (/ˈɡiːliən/, IPA-fr) is a Belgian chocolate brand and manufacturer best known for its seashell shaped pralines. The company was founded in 1958 in Sint-Niklaas, Belgium by Guy Foubert and is now owned by the South Korean company Lotte Confectionery.

Foubert and his wife formulated the original chocolate recipe and "Guylian" was formed as a portmanteau from the first names of Guy and Liliane Foubert. While Guylian specialise in roasted hazelnut pralines they produce a wide variety of products including chocolate covered fruit pieces and chocolate truffles.

The company headquarters remain in Sint-Niklaas along with their production plant but now Guylian exports internationally to approximately 120 countries. In 2003, the first Guylian Belgian Chocolate café was opened in Antwerp, Belgium; there are now 9 franchises in different locations around the world.

Guylian donates to and works with Project Seahorse to help with marine wildlife conservation and raise awareness for the charity. They have also worked with the Cocoa Horizons Foundations on Project Cocoa to sustainably source cocoa and have aimed to reduce any negative environmental impacts from their production. For this work, they have received Corporate Responsibility Awards along with multiple other awards for innovation and their chocolates.

Hugh Alan Leslie OBE (17 April 1900 – 2 September 1974) was an Australian politician. He was a member of the Country Party and served terms in the Western Australian Legislative Assembly (1943–1949) and House of Representatives (1949–1958, 1961–1963). He was a newspaper editor before entering politics.

Muhammad bin Saud Al Muqrin (Lang-ar Muḥammad bin Suʿūd Āl Muqrin; 1687–1765), also known as Ibn Saud, was the emir of Diriyah and is considered the founder of the First Saudi State and the Saud dynasty, which are named for his father, Saud bin Muhammad Al Muqrin. His reign lasted between 1727 and 1765.

Long-chain alcohol oxidase is one of two enzyme classes that oxidize long-chain or fatty alcohols to aldehydes. It has been found in certain Candida yeast, where it participates in omega oxidation of fatty acids to produce acyl-CoA for energy or industrial use, as well as in other fungi, plants, and bacteria.
Mykhailo Polyanychko (Lang-uk) (1921 – 19??) – Ukrainian politician and diplomat, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Permanent Representative of Ukraine to the United Nations.

Divya Dhayal is an archer from India. She became the national archery champion at age 15 She has represented India at world archery youth championship, archery world cups, international archery tournaments and other international archery events

She started her early archery training at the Army Sports Institute. in Pune and later at other places, under Col. Vikram S. Dhayal

"Little Muck" is a German fairy tale by Wilhelm Hauff about a small, mistreated man who gains magical slippers and a walking stick. After facing betrayal, he cleverly takes revenge on a dishonest king. The story, set in an Oriental frame narrative, critiques superficial judgment and corrupt rulers.

Lu Rongjing (born August 1933) is a Chinese politician who served as Communist Party Secretary of Anhui (1988–1996) and Governor of Anhui (1988–1989). He later chaired the Anhui Provincial Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (1996–2000). Beginning his career in the Tongguanshan Copper Mine, Lu rose through provincial leadership roles, including deputy party secretary of Tongling and Ma'anshan, before assuming top positions in Anhui's government and party apparatus.
Richard Jata (born September 21, 1985 in Dunedin, Florida) is an American soccer player.

May Song Vang (February 5, 1951 – August 5, 2013) was an American Hmong community leader and activist. She was the widow of General Vang Pao, a former member of the Royal Lao Army and prominent Hmong American leader, who died in 2011. May Song Vang became a more prominent symbol of the Hmong American community in California and the rest of the United States after the death of her husband.

Hugh Lowther, 5th Earl of Lonsdale (25 January 1857 – 13 April 1944) was a British peer, sportsman, and military officer, known as the "Yellow Earl" for his flamboyant style. He served as Lord Lieutenant of Cumberland and was a founder of the Automobile Association.

Robert Mark Altman (October 10, 1944 – September 24, 2021) was an American photographer. Altman attended Hunter College at the City University of New York and studied psychology and anthropology. Initially he had no intention of becoming a photographer, and said that the camera he wore around his neck was essentially just a prop to "meet girls". However, after graduation, he opened a shop called the Electric Lotus and displayed some of his photographs on a notice board here. The reaction here was so positive that Ansel Adams ended up taking him on as a photography apprentice.

He was soon hired as a photojournalist by Rolling Stone magazine. Here he was able to capture shots of the Rolling Stones while in the recording studio, and also captured shots of artists such as Joni Mitchell and Iggy Pop at music festivals. He found that being a photographer opened doors to places where others were not allowed, and he was able to closely follow the stars and capture images that immortalised the 1960s culture. Following his early success as chief staff photographer for Rolling Stone he expanded into fashion photography and fine art. He became a television producer/director for KEMO-TV, an independent station in the San Francisco Bay Area. For a decade beginning in the mid-1990s, Altman taught web design and photoshop as adjunct professor for several institutions including San Francisco State University and the University of California, Berkeley.

Vladimir Nikolaevich Nemukhin (Lang-ru; February 12, 1925 in village Priluki, Kaluga Oblast, RSFSR, USSR – April 18, 2016 Moscow, Russia) was a Russian artist. Nemukhin was associated with the 'Lianozovo Group' along with Oscar Rabin, Valentina Kropivnitskaya [ru] (1924-2008) and Lidiya Masterkova.

Siniperca chuatsi, commonly known as the mandarin fish, is a freshwater predatory fish native to East Asia, particularly the Amur River basin and China. It is a commercially important species, widely farmed for food and featured in traditional Chinese cuisine. Growing up to 70 cm (28 in) in length, it inhabits vegetated rivers and preys on smaller fish. First described in 1855, it is valued both ecologically and economically.
Martyrs Bus Service is a bus operator in eastern Melbourne, Australia. Founded in 1927, it operates one route with a fleet of 42 buses. It is owned by the Dineen Group.
Barrington J. Bayley (1937–2008) was a British science fiction writer associated with the New Wave movement.
Gambling Lady is a 1934 American pre-Code romantic drama film directed by Archie Mayo, and starring Barbara Stanwyck, Joel McCrea and Pat O'Brien.

Sultan of Selangor (سلطان سلاڠور) is the title of the constitutional ruler of Selangor, Malaysia who is the head of state and head of the Islamic religion in Selangor. The current monarch, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah ascended the throne on the death of his father, on 22 November 2001.
Cinéaste is an American quarterly film magazine that was established in 1967.

Joseph Buckland (born 27 October 1992) is an English ice dancer. With former partner Olivia Smart, he is a three-time British national junior champion (2012–14) and competed at three World Junior Championships, reaching the top ten in 2014.

Rupa Goswami (Lang-sa, Lang-bn, IAST: Rūpa Gosvāmī; 1489–1564) was a devotional teacher (guru), poet, and philosopher of the Gaudiya Vaishnava tradition. With his brother Sanatana Goswami, he is considered the most senior of the six Goswamis of Vrindavan associated with Caitanya Mahaprabhu, a hidden avatar (incarnation) of Krishna in Kali Yuga.

Clifford B. Harmon (July 1, 1866 – June 25, 1945) was an American real-estate developer and early aviation pioneer. He founded the Harmon Trophy for outstanding achievements in aviation and developed Harmon-on-Hudson, now part of Croton-on-Hudson, New York.

Yossi Gross (Hebrew: יוסי גרוס; born February 5, 1947) is an Israeli entrepreneur, inventor, and engineer known for founding numerous medical device companies. Born in Budapest, Hungary, he immigrated to Israel as a child. After earning an MSc in aeronautical engineering from the Technion, he worked at Israel Aircraft Industries on the Lavi fighter jet project.  

In the 1990s, Gross developed drug delivery systems at Elan Medical Technologies before launching multiple medical startups. In 2007, he co-founded Rainbow Medical, a venture incubator focused on early-stage medical innovations, including treatments for cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and ophthalmology. Gross holds numerous patents and has collaborated with major firms like Medtronic and Boston Scientific. He is also a member of the Israeli Life Science Association.

MyPertamina is a digital financial service platform from Pertamina that integrated with the apps LinkAja. This application is used for non-cash fuel oil payments at Pertamina's public fueling stations.

Jean Messiha (IPA-fr; born Hossam Boutros Messiha, Lang-ar‎, 10 September 1970) is an Egyptian-born French economist, politician and former senior civil servant. He was appointed Deputy Undersecretary of Management at the Ministry of Defence in 2014 and joined the National Front (FN) in 2016, when he became spokesman of Horaces, a group of high-ranking civil servants and business executives who meet once a month to discuss the party platform. Messiha stood as a candidate in the 2017 legislative election in the 4th constituency of Aisne. In 2020, he left the party to assume the presidency of the Apollon Institute, a far-right think tank. In 2022, Messiha joined Éric Zemmour's newly-founded Reconquête party and became its spokesman, but he since has left the party afterwards.

The 2000 Save Mart/Kragen 350 was the 16th stock car race of the 2000 NASCAR Winston Cup Series and the 12th iteration of the event. The race was held on Sunday, June 25, 2000, in Sonoma, California, at the club layout in Sears Point Raceway, a 1.99 miles (3.20 km) permanent road course layout. The race took the scheduled 112 laps to complete. At race's end, Hendrick Motorsports driver Jeff Gordon would dominate the late stages of a chaotic race to win his 51st career NASCAR Winston Cup Series win and his first of the season. To fill out the podium, Sterling Marlin of Team SABCO and Mark Martin of Roush Racing would finish second and third, respectively.

John William Gleeson (14 March 1938 – 7 October 2016) was an Australian cricketer who played 29 Test matches as a leg-spinner between 1967 and 1972. Known for his unusual two-fingered grip, he took 93 Test wickets, including three five-wicket hauls. He later worked for Telecom Australia and served on World Series Cricket's governing committee. Gleeson died in Tamworth, New South Wales, aged 78.

Harry Kroger (d. September 2022) was an American physicist and electrical engineer. He earned his Ph.D. from Cornell University in 1962 and worked in industrial research at Sperry Research Center and the Microelectronics and Computer Technology Corporation (MCC). Later, he became a professor at Binghamton University, where he directed the Integrated Electronics Engineering Center. Kroger held numerous patents in superconductors and semiconductor technology and authored over 60 research papers.

The Forging of a Rebel is an autobiographical trilogy of books written by the Spanish author, broadcaster and journalist Arturo Barea. They were first published during the author's exile in the United Kingdom after the Spanish Civil War.

Lectionary 34, designated by siglum l 34 (in the Gregory-Aland numbering). It is a Greek manuscript of the New Testament, on parchment leaves. Palaeographically it has been assigned to the 9th century. The manuscript is lacunose.

The Sultan of Selangor is the constitutional monarch and head of Islam in the Malaysian state of Selangor. The current ruler, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, has reigned since 2001. The Selangor Sultanate, established in 1766, traces its origins to Bugis nobility. The Sultan holds executive authority, appoints the Menteri Besar, and plays a key role in the Conference of Rulers, which elects Malaysia's Yang di-Pertuan Agong. Two Selangor sultans have served as Yang di-Pertuan Agong. Succession follows male primogeniture.

Lectionary 34 (Gregory-Aland), also known as Codex Monacensis 329, is a 9th-century Greek Gospel lectionary manuscript written in uncial script on parchment. It contains Gospel readings with one lacuna and is held at the Bavarian State Library in Munich.

Conrad Albert 'Connie' Lau (February 8, 1921 – April 18, 1964) was an American aeronautical engineer, inventor, and executive. Lau led or contributed to the development of a number of important aircraft and spacecraft projects.

Connect Four is a two-player connection game in which players take turns dropping colored discs into a vertical grid, aiming to be the first to form a horizontal, vertical, or diagonal line of four of their own discs. Designed by Howard Wexler and Ned Strongin and first published by Milton Bradley in 1974, the game is played on a 7-column, 6-row board. Mathematically solved in 1988, the first player can force a win with perfect play. Numerous variations exist, including different board sizes, rule modifications like *PopOut* and *Pop 10*, and themed editions. The game has been adapted into digital formats, arcade versions, and oversized physical sets, maintaining its popularity as a classic strategy game.
